identity.variant: Calvin-40 / current Calvin industrial humanoid platform

positioningFacts.value: Wandercraft positions Calvin as a heavy-duty carrier robot that moves heavy loads, handles boxes and crates, and picks small parts with precision; it is described as fully autonomous and intended for factories and logistics hubs.
The current Calvin page says it is designed for heavy loads and precision tasks, is deployed in actual production, and is the first humanoid deployed in Europe for assembly-line production tasks.

Calvin is trained on 12 generic industrial/logistics use cases, three of which the official page says are already deployed; the page describes continuous operation from 8 to 22 hours per day, not a per-charge battery runtime.

identity.variant: MenteeBot V3 / third-generation vertically integrated humanoid robot

positioningFacts.value: Mentee describes MenteeBot as a vertically integrated humanoid robot with self-made actuators, onboard sensing/drivers, bespoke electronics and redundant fail-safe compute.
The official product page publishes 175cm height, 70kg weight, 1.5m/s speed, up to 25kg carrying capacity, 40 DoF and voice interaction.
Mobileye describes autonomous task execution without teleoperation, hot-swappable batteries, 24/7 operational availability and planned customer POCs in 2026 with commercialization targeted for 2028.

identity.variant: G1 general-purpose mobile dual-arm robot / current G1 documentation version 2.0

positioningFacts.value: Galbot positions G1 as a mobile dual-arm general-purpose robot using a 360-degree omnidirectional-wheel chassis, adaptive height operation and long-reach manipulation.
The official G1 page publishes 1730mm standard posture height, 92.5kg body weight, 1.5m/s maximum speed, 48V 30Ah battery, 8h full-charge runtime, IP54 and AGX Orin 64GB 275TOPS.
Galbot's official developer platform publishes GalbotSDK, G1 documentation and solution packages for reception/guidance, picking and inspection workflows.

rawValue.rawValue: Aeolus offers aeo bundled solutions and flexible leasing/subscription plans; RaaS includes deployment, operation and maintenance rather than a public unit purchase price
rawValue.evidenceSummary: 現行公式のsubscription/RaaS導線と運用支援を確認。

unresolvedProcurement.reason: 公式ページを再確認したが、conflict/variantまたは取得状態を解消できない。
unresolvedProcurement.currentValue: Aeolusは継続支援、保守、修理、ソフトウェア更新を案内。丸文の国内窓口は確認できるが、現行aeoの販売・保守条件は要確認。

conflicts: Current Aeolus product pages use the name aeo, while Japanese deployment releases from 2020 and 2022 use the generic name Aeolus Robot and do not specify a generation; deployment examples are linked but current-variant equivalence remains unconfirmed.
Repository contains a 150cm height and current Japan-support assertion without a current official aeo numeric/spec source; these values were not used as found evidence.
humanReviewRequired: Confirm whether the 2020/2022 Japanese deployment units are the current aeo variant or an earlier Aeolus Robot generation.
Confirm current aeo height, weight, speed, DoF, payload, battery and safety documentation with Aeolus/Marubun before public numeric display.
